title: Current parameter const, mutability, and declarator spelling
summary: Concrete and deduced lvalue-reference parameters are const-default, while pointer pointee and pointer-object constness stay explicit.
details: const Foo& input becomes input: Foo&; absence of mut means read-only.
Foo& output becomes output: mut Foo&.
const auto& input becomes input&, while auto& output becomes mut output&.
const T* input remains input: const T^ and T* output remains output: T^; pointer pointee mutability is not inferred into the binding marker.
T* const fixed becomes const fixed: T^ and const T* const fixed becomes const fixed: const T^, preserving pointer-object and pointee constness as separate axes.
Reference spacing is canonicalized: T& x, T &x, and T & x all render as x: T&.
Concrete name: type parameters in a multiline parameter list align every colon to the widest absolute visible binding column; auto-reference parameters have no colon and do not participate.

If a continuation row contains several parameters, every complete parameter on that row is flipped compactly; list-wide vertical colon padding is disabled because several colons cannot share one absolute column.
An explicit-const comparison profile remains available only for scratch style-lab buffers; ordinary source uses const_default.

provenance.note: The owner selected the const-default rendering after viewing actual Neovim captures.
The production profile and parameter renderer implement the complete current rule.

evidence.claim: Concrete/deduced references, pointer const axes, source-spacing normalization, and buffer-local comparison profiles are pinned.
Integrated header rendering uses the selected const-default spelling.

questions: How do const and mutable reference arguments render now?
How are const T*, T* const, and const T* const distinguished?
Does LLVM-style T &name keep its whitespace?
Where do colons align in a multiline function argument list?

last_verified.method: Owner-selected profile plus aligned and packed-row layouts verified by focused, interaction, golden, and real-grid captures.
